site.title: ifuri Connect - URI connector hub for urirun
site.description: Install ifuri and urirun connectors from one catalog. Each connector documents its URI schemes, routes, requirements, install command and registry contract.

connectors.name: Planfile Tasks
connectors.summary: Plan, group and execute daily tasks through task:// and planfile:// URI commands.
connectors.description: Planfile Tasks is an external urirun connector package that turns a local planfile project into URI-addressable work items. It owns ticket list, create, status and DSL routes, generates bindings from decorators, compiles into a normal urirun registry, and projects to MCP tools / A2A skills for host workflows.
useCases: Create tasks from chat or a host workflow.
Group tasks into sprint-like queues.
Change ticket lifecycle state from a URI flow.
Run planfile DSL from the same URI registry as other automation.
Expose task operations to MCP/A2A so LLM agents can discover and call them.

payload.name: Check DNS records
payload.prompt: Check if configured domains resolve and answer with HTTP 200

connectors.name: SQLite Context Store
connectors.summary: Local data, artifacts, checks and logs over data://, artifact://, check:// and log://.
connectors.description: SQLite Context Store is an external urirun connector package that gives the host a small durable memory for records, artifacts, checks and logs. It generates bindings from decorators, compiles into a normal urirun registry, and projects to MCP tools / A2A skills for host workflows that need to remember what was checked, imported, generated or changed.
useCases: Store structured context imported by an LLM.
Create datasets and upsert records from URI flows.
List artifacts created by automation.
Show backend and node logs in the host UI.
Expose local context to MCP/A2A without coupling the app to SQLite APIs.

connectors.name: Domain Monitor
connectors.summary: HTTP, DNS reads, screenshots, logs and daily domain checks through URI commands.
connectors.description: Domain Monitor is an external urirun connector package for recurring website operations. It generates bindings from decorators, checks HTTP and DNS state, writes logs, stores screenshot metadata and exposes daily check flows through the same URI registry used by MCP and A2A. Provider-specific DNS mutations live in dedicated connectors such as Namecheap DNS.
useCases: Check if a site responds correctly.
Compare expected and current DNS records.
Record logs and screenshot artifacts from the same domain workflow.
Compose domain checks with provider-specific DNS connectors when a repair is needed.
Expose domain operations to MCP/A2A so LLM agents can discover and call them.

connectors.name: HTTP Check
connectors.summary: Check HTTP endpoints through httpcheck:// URI commands and a small Python CLI.
connectors.description: HTTP Check is a minimal external urirun connector package. It verifies that a URL responds, optionally checks an expected status code, and returns structured JSON for host workflows, smoke tests and daily operations.
useCases: Check that a website responds with HTTP 200.
Use a small external connector package in ifuri flows.
Prove that connect.ifuri.com can install connector packages from GitHub.

connectors.name: Time Tools
connectors.summary: Expose current time and timezone checks through time:// URI commands.
connectors.description: Time Tools is a minimal external urirun connector package. It declares time:// routes with @connector.command, returns structured JSON, and can be projected to MCP tools / A2A skills.
useCases: Stamp task and flow execution with host-local or UTC time.
Verify that connector packages can be installed from GitHub and projected to MCP/A2A.
Use time:// in flows without coupling the flow to Python code.

connectors.name: Namecheap DNS
connectors.summary: Safe DNS query, plan, backup and apply flow for Namecheap host records.
connectors.description: Namecheap DNS is a dedicated connector package for Namecheap host-record operations. Workflows can query current records, render expected records, plan changes, back up the current host set and apply a reviewed DNS update without hard-coding SDK calls in the host app.
useCases: Read current Namecheap host records from the API or mock payloads.
Render expected A/AAAA records for domain-check flows.
Generate a DNS change plan from desired records.
Back up Namecheap host records before a change.
Apply reviewed DNS records from a host workflow.

connectors.name: gRPC Transport
connectors.summary: Expose and call URI registries over gRPC for service-to-service communication.
connectors.description: gRPC Transport is a transport connector for exposing URI registries between services. It keeps the same URI binding contract while switching the network transport to gRPC.
useCases: Expose a runtime registry to another service.
Use URI routes over gRPC instead of only HTTP.
Test service-to-service URI execution contracts.

connectors.name: MQTT Device Bridge
connectors.summary: Publish, subscribe and bridge firmware/backend/frontend device commands through MQTT URI routes.
connectors.description: MQTT Device Bridge will map device, firmware, backend and frontend actions to shared URI routes. The goal is to control firmware and read telemetry without a separate SDK per layer.
useCases: Publish firmware commands from a backend flow.
Use the same device URI from frontend and host.
Bridge telemetry into the registry runtime.

connectors.name: Browser Control
connectors.summary: Drive browser/noVNC actions through browser:// URI commands for demos and QA.
connectors.description: Browser Control exposes browser actions as URI commands. By default it is safe and does not open the physical host browser; set BROWSER_CONTROL_ENDPOINT or URI_SERVICE_MAP to forward commands to a noVNC or urirun node.
useCases: Open pages inside a controlled noVNC demo desktop.
Capture browser screenshots as URI flow artifacts.
Run repeatable browser QA from NL-to-URI workflows.
requires: browser runtime or noVNC urirun node

connectors.summary: Control Android Debug Bridge devices through URI routes.
connectors.description: ADB is an if-uri urirun connector for control Android Debug Bridge devices through URI routes. It is represented in the public catalog so the connector can be discovered, versioned and linked to its repository while implementation details are completed.
useCases: Discover ADB capabilities from the connector catalog.
Plan flows that use adb:// URI routes.
Install or track the connector from its GitHub repository.

useCases: Discover Adopt capabilities from the connector catalog.

connectors.description: Agents is an if-uri urirun connector for expose local agent discovery and execution through URI routes. It is represented in the public catalog so the connector can be discovered, versioned and linked to its repository while implementation details are completed.

connectors.name: Base64 (PHP)
connectors.summary: Encode and decode text with Base64 through codec:// URI commands.
connectors.description: Base64 is a polyglot urirun connector written in PHP. It proves connectors are language-agnostic: the script emits a v2 bindings document and answers the codec:// route via an argv-template the urirun runtime invokes.
useCases: Encode or decode payloads inside a URI flow without a Python dependency.
Demonstrate that urirun connectors can be authored in PHP.
Project a PHP connector to MCP tools / A2A skills like any other connector.

connectors.name: Calendar
connectors.summary: Read and create calendar events through URI routes.
connectors.description: Calendar is an if-uri urirun connector for read and create calendar events through URI routes. It is represented in the public catalog so the connector can be discovered, versioned and linked to its repository while implementation details are completed.
useCases: Discover Calendar capabilities from the connector catalog.
Plan flows that use calendar:// URI routes.
Install or track the connector from its GitHub repository.

connectors.name: Camera capture + OCR
connectors.summary: Turn a USB/built-in webcam into data over camera:// URIs: discover cameras, beep before scan, capture a still frame, describe what is in the photo, find and crop to the dominant object, OCR it, and run simple inspection/alert rules. Capture uses ffmpeg (OpenCV optional); scene understanding + object detection reuse the img2nl engine via urirun-connector-ocr (Pillow edge/stats fallback); OCR uses tesseract, upgrading to the richer OCR connector backends automatically.
connectors.description: camera:// is the capture+understand layer on top of usb:// camera discovery. camera://host/devices/query/list lists cameras (product names + /dev/video* nodes via the usb connector). camera://host/photo/command/capture grabs one still frame and can emit a pre-scan beep so a human knows exactly when scanning happens. camera://host/photo/query/describe returns a natural-language description of what is in the photo — scene, dominant colours, detected objects/regions, barcodes — using the img2nl engine when available. camera://host/photo/query/analyze runs the full pipeline: capture (or reuse a given image), describe it, detect the dominant object (img2nl region detector, else Pillow edge density), crop to it ('dociąć do obiektu'), and OCR the crop — returning the photo, description, object bbox + crop path, and recognised text. camera://host/photo/query/inspect adds simple rule checks (required/forbidden text, min OCR length, object required, brightness range), returns structured alerts with optional beep_on_alert/fail_on_alert, and persists a verdict (inspection.json sidecar + optional JSONL audit_log) so alerting has a durable trail without a separate log connector. camera://host/photo/query/compare does change/motion detection — compare two files, a reference vs a fresh frame, or two frames captured interval_ms apart — returning a change ratio, a changed flag and the changed region, with optional beep_on_change/fail_on_change to trigger scan-on-motion or stop-on-change flows. camera://host/photo/query/ocr just returns the text. Dependency-light core (ffmpeg + Pillow + tesseract); img2nl/imgl and OpenCV are optional upgrades.
useCases: Capture a webcam frame and OCR it.
Describe what is in a photo — scene, objects, barcodes.
Inspect a scanned item against rules and raise alerts.
Detect change/motion between two frames.

connectors.name: Camera Web
connectors.summary: Capture frames and status from browser-accessible cameras.
connectors.description: Camera Web is an if-uri urirun connector for capture frames and status from browser-accessible cameras. It is represented in the public catalog so the connector can be discovered, versioned and linked to its repository while implementation details are completed.
useCases: Discover Camera Web capabilities from the connector catalog.
Plan flows that use camera:// URI routes.
Install or track the connector from its GitHub repository.

connectors.name: Chat Bridge
connectors.summary: Deprecated chat bridge — map transcript to URI and forward to urisys RDP stack.
connectors.description: Deprecated chat bridge — map transcript to URI and forward to urisys RDP stack. A tellmesh capability pack adopted into urirun via its manifest (`urirun adopt-pack`): 2 chat:// routes mapped 1:1 to bindings.v2, validated, ready to project to MCP tools / A2A skills.
useCases: Address chat:// capabilities as URIs in a flow.
Project Chat Bridge operations to MCP tools / A2A skills.
Adopt the pack with one command, no code change.
requires: the tellmesh 'urichat' pack (GitHub) for real handlers; dispatch works simulated without it

connectors.name: Connectorgen
connectors.summary: Generate connector scaffolds and validate connector manifests.
connectors.description: Connectorgen is an if-uri urirun connector for generate connector scaffolds and validate connector manifests. It is represented in the public catalog so the connector can be discovered, versioned and linked to its repository while implementation details are completed.
useCases: Discover Connectorgen capabilities from the connector catalog.
Plan flows that use connectorgen:// URI routes.

connectors.name: Document text / OCR
connectors.summary: Extract text from PDFs and images over doc:// URIs. Text-layer PDFs use pdftotext; scans/images fall back through an OCR chain (tesseract, RapidOCR/ONNX from wronai-img2nl, or an Ollama vision model from wronai-ocr). Degrades gracefully to pdftotext-only.
connectors.description: doc:// turns document text extraction into a first-class URI instead of ad-hoc shell + pdftotext. doc://host/file/query/text returns the text of a PDF or image: a text-layer PDF goes through poppler pdftotext; a scan/image falls back to OCR, rasterising PDF pages (pdftoppm/pdfimages/magick) and running the first available engine — tesseract, then RapidOCR (the ONNX engine reused from wronai/img2nl, no system binary needed), then an Ollama vision model (reused from wronai/ocr). doc://host/file/query/ocr forces OCR; doc://host/engine/query/list reports which engines are present. Useful for classifying invoices and any scanned document over the mesh.
useCases: Extract the text layer of a PDF inside a flow.
OCR a scanned invoice or image that has no text layer.
Check which OCR engines are available on a node.

system: pdftotext (poppler) recommended; pdftoppm/pdfimages/magick for OCR rasterisation
optional: rapidocr-onnxruntime or img2nl for no-tesseract OCR

connectors.name: Docid
connectors.summary: Identify and tag documents with stable document IDs.
connectors.description: Docid is an if-uri urirun connector for identify and tag documents with stable document IDs. It is represented in the public catalog so the connector can be discovered, versioned and linked to its repository while implementation details are completed.
useCases: Discover Docid capabilities from the connector catalog.

connectors.name: Document

connectors.description: Document is an if-uri urirun connector for read, convert and inspect document files through URI routes. It is represented in the public catalog so the connector can be discovered, versioned and linked to its repository while implementation details are completed.

connectors.name: Email
connectors.summary: Read inbox, send mail, and extract local Thunderbird invoice attachments through email:// URI routes (IMAP + SMTP + local mbox).
connectors.description: Email is an external urirun connector that exposes IMAP reads (inbox list, message read), SMTP send, and local Thunderbird mailbox extraction as email:// routes. The IMAP/SMTP routes touch a remote mail server, so they are gated by urirun execute policy. The local/thunderbird route reads mbox files from one profile or auto-discovered Thunderbird profile roots, extracts invoice-like attachments into monthly YYYY.MM folders, deduplicates attachment payloads, supports dry-run, and can write a JSON report. Credentials for remote routes come from the environment (EMAIL_IMAP_HOST/EMAIL_SMTP_HOST/EMAIL_USER/EMAIL_PASS), never the manifest.
useCases: List the inbox inside a flow.
Send a notification email.
Extract invoice attachments from local Thunderbird.

connectors.name: Environment
connectors.summary: Controlled env:// runtime configuration and secrets.
connectors.description: Controlled env:// runtime configuration and secrets. A tellmesh capability pack adopted into urirun via its manifest (`urirun adopt-pack`): 9 env:// routes mapped 1:1 to bindings.v2, validated.
useCases: Address env:// capabilities as URIs.
Project Environment to MCP/A2A.
Adopt with one command, no code change.
requires: the tellmesh 'urienv' pack (GitHub) for real handlers

connectors.name: Flow Repair
connectors.summary: Generate a urirun flow (YAML) from natural language, execute it, and self-repair from failures via an LLM.
connectors.description: Flow Repair is an external urirun connector that turns a natural-language goal into a urirun flow as YAML, validates every step URI against the registry action space, runs the steps under policy, and — when a step fails — feeds the structured error back to an LLM to obtain a corrected flow, then re-runs (up to `tries`). LLM model/provider selection is delegated to the `llm` connector (provider-prefixed models go through litellm; bare models hit Ollama). Steps execute only with `--execute` / `mode=execute`.
useCases: Generate a urirun flow (YAML) from natural language.
Execute a flow and self-repair from failures.
Iterate a flow until it passes.

payload.goal: check all sites and email me the failures

connectors.name: Filesystem duplicates
connectors.summary: Find duplicate files in any folder over fs:// URIs. Exact byte duplicates by SHA-256 (stdlib) and near-duplicate IMAGES by perceptual hash (reusing wronai-img2nl analyze_fingerprint). A move command quarantines extras into _duplicates/, keeping the first of each group.
connectors.description: fs:// turns duplicate detection into a first-class URI instead of ad-hoc shell + sha256sum. fs://host/duplicates/query/find scans a folder tree and returns groups of duplicate files: mode=sha256 finds byte-identical files of any type; mode=perceptual finds near-duplicate images by perceptual hash (phash/dhash/whash via the wronai/img2nl fingerprint engine, compared by Hamming distance within a threshold). fs://host/duplicates/command/move quarantines the extra copies (all but the first of each group) into <root>/_duplicates/<key>/, with a dry_run default. Built for the office flow — dedupe exported invoices and attachments.
useCases: Find byte-identical duplicate files in a folder tree.
Detect near-duplicate images by perceptual hash.
Quarantine the extra copies of exported invoices.

optional: pillow + imagehash (or img2nl) for perceptual image mode

connectors.name: Get Node
connectors.summary: Bootstrap and health-check urirun nodes from get.ifuri.com through node:// URI routes.
connectors.description: Get Node wraps the get.ifuri.com installer so flows can fetch the node bootstrap script and check a node's health over node:// routes — a programmatic path to stand up and verify urirun nodes across a LAN.
useCases: Fetch the node installer script in a provisioning flow.
Check a freshly installed node's /health.
Stand up urirun nodes across a LAN from one host.
requires: get.ifuri.com reachable; target host with python3

connectors.name: GitHub
connectors.summary: Clone git repos and deploy them anywhere via bindings/registry over github:// URI routes.
connectors.description: GitHub is an external urirun connector that bootstraps projects onto any machine over the URI contract: clone a repo, pull updates, pip install it, and emit its urirun_bindings() so the project can be compiled into a registry and served or host-deployed without logging into the box. Clones land under URIRUN_PROJECTS (default ~/.urirun-projects).
useCases: Clone a git repo over a URI in a flow.
Install a package straight from a GitHub repo.
Inspect a repo's urirun bindings/registry.

connectors.name: Hash (Go)
connectors.summary: Compute SHA-256 digests of text through hash:// URI commands.
connectors.description: Hash is a polyglot urirun connector written in Go. It proves connectors are language-agnostic: a single static binary emits a v2 bindings document and answers the hash:// route via an argv-template the urirun runtime invokes.
useCases: Fingerprint payloads inside a URI flow without a Python dependency.
Demonstrate that urirun connectors can be authored in Go and shipped as one binary.
Project a Go connector to MCP tools / A2A skills like any other connector.

connectors.description: Human input mock (mouse/keyboard) for urisys-node. A tellmesh capability pack adopted into urirun via its manifest (`urirun adopt-pack`): 8 him:// routes mapped 1:1 to bindings.v2, validated, ready to project to MCP tools / A2A skills.
useCases: Address him:// capabilities as URIs in a flow.
Project Human-in-the-Middle operations to MCP tools / A2A skills.
Adopt the pack with one command, no code change.

connectors.summary: Screen capture + UI target detection via img2nl (KVM eyes).
connectors.description: Screen capture + UI target detection via img2nl (KVM eyes). A tellmesh capability pack adopted into urirun via its manifest (`urirun adopt-pack`): 3 img2nl:// routes mapped 1:1 to bindings.v2, validated, ready to project to MCP tools / A2A skills.

connectors.name: Invoice field extraction
connectors.summary: Extract structured fields from invoices over invoice:// URIs — NIP, number, dates, net/VAT/gross, seller, currency. Regex tuned for Polish faktura (also EN), text via pdftotext, optional LLM (OpenRouter) fallback. A folder route batches a month of invoices into a CSV with net/VAT/gross totals.
connectors.description: invoice:// turns invoice parsing into a first-class URI. invoice://host/file/query/parse returns structured fields {nip, number, issueDate, seller, net, vat, gross, currency} from one PDF/text — regex-first (text-layer PDFs via pdftotext), with an optional LLM pass (OpenRouter via litellm, use_llm=true) for layouts the regex misses. invoice://host/folder/query/audit parses every invoice under a folder into rows plus net/VAT/gross totals, optionally writing a CSV — for monthly accounting. Read-only over the invoice files. Pairs with doc:// (OCR for scans) and fs:// (dedupe) in the office flow.
useCases: Extract NIP, dates and net/VAT/gross from one invoice.
Audit a folder of invoices into rows plus monthly totals.
Feed parsed rows into a spreadsheet for accounting.

optional: litellm + OPENROUTER_API_KEY for the LLM fallback

connectors.name: Koru
connectors.summary: Integrate Koru resources with URI-addressable status and sync routes.
connectors.description: Koru is an if-uri urirun connector for integrate Koru resources with URI-addressable status and sync routes. It is represented in the public catalog so the connector can be discovered, versioned and linked to its repository while implementation details are completed.
useCases: Discover Koru capabilities from the connector catalog.
Plan flows that use koru:// URI routes.
Install or track the connector from its GitHub repository.

connectors.name: KSeF 2.0
connectors.summary: Polish national e-invoicing (KSeF 2.0) as ksef:// routes: auth, send invoice, query, download.
connectors.description: KSeF 2.0 reference connector. The HTTP surface is declarative (urirun fetch adapter, environments test/demo/prod). The ksef-token auth handshake (challenge -> RSA-OAEP(token|timestamp) -> poll -> redeem) and client-side crypto (AES-256-CBC + RSAES-OAEP, SHA-256 invoice hash) are the two imperative helpers. The access token is addressed by reference (getv://KSEF_ACCESS_TOKEN), never embedded. Smoke against the TEST environment with random NIPs.
useCases: Authenticate with a KSeF token and obtain a JWT access token.
Encrypt and send an FA(3) invoice in an online session, then poll acceptance/UPO.
Query accepted invoices and download by ksefNumber.

connectors.name: KVM Control
connectors.summary: Send keyboard/mouse input and capture the screen through kvm:// URI routes.
connectors.description: KVM Control maps remote keyboard, mouse and screen-capture actions to kvm:// routes so a host can drive another machine over the URI contract, gated by policy. Intended for lab automation and assisted remote control, not unattended access.
useCases: Type text into a remote machine from a flow.
Move/click the pointer for assisted control.
Capture the remote screen for a check.
requires: a host that grants input-control permissions (e.g. uinput / accessibility)

connectors.description: Local LLM bridges a self-hosted model runtime (Ollama, llama.cpp or vLLM) into llm:// routes, so flows and agents can request completions and list models with the same URI contract — and project them to MCP tools / A2A skills, keeping inference on-device.

Keep inference on the LAN with no external API.

connectors.name: MCP Filesystem
connectors.summary: Read, list and write files through fs:// URI routes backed by the MCP filesystem server.
connectors.description: MCP Filesystem bridges the Model Context Protocol filesystem server (@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem) into urirun fs:// routes, so flows and agents can read, list and write files under an allowlisted root with the same URI contract — and project them to MCP tools / A2A skills.
useCases: Read a file into a flow step under an allowlisted root.
List a directory from an agent via MCP tools.
Write generated artifacts back to disk through a policy-gated route.

connectors.name: PDF
connectors.summary: Render markdown or HTML to PDF and read PDF page count/metadata through pdf:// URI routes. Headless, no GUI or office suite.
connectors.description: PDF is an external urirun connector that exposes headless PDF rendering as pdf:// routes. It renders markdown text/files (markdown -> HTML -> PDF) and HTML strings/files to PDF using weasyprint when available, falling back to reportlab for basic text output. A read-only query returns the page count and document metadata of an existing PDF via pypdf (or PyPDF2). All third-party imports are lazy, so the connector imports cleanly with none of the rendering libraries installed, and every handler returns a urirun envelope instead of raising.
useCases: Render a generated markdown report to a shareable PDF inside a flow.
Convert an HTML template to PDF headlessly.
Inspect page count and metadata of an existing PDF.

connectors.name: RDP Desktop
connectors.summary: RDP session and X11 display queries for urirdp-docker.
connectors.description: RDP session and X11 display queries for urirdp-docker. A tellmesh capability pack adopted into urirun via its manifest (`urirun adopt-pack`): 5 rdp:// routes mapped 1:1 to bindings.v2, validated, ready to project to MCP tools / A2A skills.
useCases: Address rdp:// capabilities as URIs in a flow.
Project RDP Desktop operations to MCP tools / A2A skills.

requires: the tellmesh 'urirdp' pack (GitHub) for real handlers; dispatch works simulated without it

connectors.name: Screen Capture
connectors.summary: Screen capture — frame query, single capture, capture loop.
connectors.description: Screen capture — frame query, single capture, capture loop. A tellmesh capability pack adopted into urirun via its manifest (`urirun adopt-pack`): 3 screen:// routes mapped 1:1 to bindings.v2, validated, ready to project to MCP tools / A2A skills.
useCases: Address screen:// capabilities as URIs in a flow.
Project Screen Capture operations to MCP tools / A2A skills.
Adopt the pack with one command, no code change.
requires: the tellmesh 'uriscreen' pack (GitHub) for real handlers; dispatch works simulated without it

connectors.name: Spreadsheet I/O (XLSX/CSV)
connectors.summary: Read and write XLSX/CSV over sheet:// URIs without libreoffice. CSV via stdlib (always works); XLSX via openpyxl (pure Python). Reads return {columns, rows}; writes take rows and pick CSV/XLSX from the extension, with an optional totals footer. Pairs with invoice://ksef:// to emit accountant-ready Excel.
connectors.description: sheet:// makes spreadsheet I/O a first-class URI. sheet://host/file/query/read returns {columns, rows} from a .xlsx (any tab) or .csv — for ingesting bank statements and accounting exports. sheet://host/file/command/write writes a list of row dicts to .xlsx or .csv (chosen by extension), with an optional per-column SUM footer — for turning parsed invoice / KSeF VAT-register rows into an Excel an accountant opens. sheet://host/file/query/info lists sheets + dimensions. CSV needs no extra deps; XLSX needs openpyxl (pip).
useCases: Read a bank statement or accounting export as columns + rows.
Write parsed invoice / VAT-register rows to an XLSX an accountant opens.
List the sheets and dimensions of a workbook.

connectors.name: USB devices
connectors.summary: Enumerate and classify the USB devices plugged into a computer over usb:// URIs. Reads the kernel's sysfs view (no root, no extra deps), labels each device by USB class codes (camera/webcam, keyboard, mouse, audio, storage, hub, printer, bluetooth, serial, smart-card/security-key) and maps it to the /dev nodes it owns (/dev/video*, /dev/input/*, /dev/ttyUSB*, /dev/hidraw*).
connectors.description: usb:// answers 'what is physically connected to this machine?' as a first-class URI instead of ad-hoc lsusb parsing. usb://host/devices/query/list returns every device with a friendly category, roles, vendor/product ids and the device nodes it exposes. usb://host/devices/query/find searches by name, vendor:product or category. usb://host/cameras/query/list returns only USB cameras with their /dev/video* capture nodes — the discovery layer the camera connector builds on. usb://host/input/query/list returns keyboards, mice and other HID gear with their /dev/input nodes. usb://host/devices/query/probe reports host support. Built on Linux sysfs (/sys/bus/usb/devices) and /sys/class/* node mapping; lsusb is used only to fill in nicer vendor/product names.
useCases: List every USB device with a friendly category.
Find USB cameras and their /dev/video* nodes.
Enumerate keyboards and mice with their /dev/input nodes.

connectors.name: UUID (JavaScript)
connectors.summary: Generate UUID v4 identifiers through uuid:// URI commands.
connectors.description: UUID is a polyglot urirun connector written in JavaScript (Node). It proves connectors are language-agnostic: it emits a v2 bindings document and answers the uuid:// route via an argv-template the urirun runtime invokes.
useCases: Mint correlation ids inside a URI flow without a Python dependency.
Demonstrate that urirun connectors can be authored in JavaScript.
Project a JS connector to MCP tools / A2A skills like any other connector.
